message:
fix(target_home): drop /home/$user heuristic from 14 more *_resolve_current_home / *_home_for_user helpers; *_home_for_user now falls back to $HOME only when user == current_user

Continuation of the prior `fix(run-as-user): drop /home/$user
heuristic` commit, applied to the next layer of helper libraries
that grew the same defensive resolver pattern.  Each of these
files defined its own `<lib>_resolve_current_home()` and (where
applicable) `<lib>_home_for_user()` because the libs are dlopen-
style sourced into independent script processes and can't share
state.

Resolvers stripped of the `/home/$user` heuristic
-------------------------------------------------
scripts/lib/{changelog,cheatsheet,continue,dashboard,doctor,
doctor_fix,export-config,info,notifications,notify,smoke_test,
state,status,support,webhook}.sh
  Removed the trailing
    `if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
        printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"; return 0
     fi`
  block from each `<lib>_resolve_current_home()` resolver.  Same
  rationale as the prior commit: the regex-shaped guess produced
  /home/<typoed-user> directories and silently routed sudo into
  the wrong home when getent and the current-user shortcut both
  failed.  Now they fail closed and let the caller's "Invalid
  TARGET_HOME for '<user>': <empty>" assertion fire.

`<lib>_home_for_user()` fallback narrowed
------------------------------------------
For the libs that also define an arbitrary-user resolver
(`<lib>_home_for_user "<user>"`) — used when the lib needs the
home dir of a user *other* than the running one — replaced the
prior "regex-matches → /home/$user" final branch with a tighter
fallback: ONLY when the requested user equals the running
process's effective user (`id -un` / `whoami`), use the existing
sanitized $HOME.  Otherwise return failure.  This preserves the
self-resolution shortcut without ever inventing a path for a
foreign user.

Each call site that already used the `<lib>_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path`
helper for the same purpose stays unchanged.

scripts/lib/changelog.sh — `resolve_changelog_acfs_home` order
swap
  Reordered the candidate priority so a script-detected ACFS_HOME
  (the directory containing state.json/VERSION/CHANGELOG.md) wins
  over `$_CHANGELOG_EXPLICIT_ACFS_HOME` env var.  Reason: the
  explicit env var was being inherited from a wrapper shell long
  after the user changed ACFS_HOME on disk, and the script-
  detection path picks up the current installation reliably.  If
  no script-detected home is available, the explicit env var is
  still consulted as a fallback.

scripts/lib/export-config.sh — additional refactor (49 LoC delta)
  Picked up the same fallback-narrowing changes as the other libs
  PLUS a small consolidation of three near-duplicate
  resolution helpers into a shared inner function.  No behavioural
  change beyond the failure-mode tightening above.

tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh — +257 LoC of cases
  New cases exercising:
    - Each lib's `<lib>_resolve_current_home()` returns failure
      when getent fails and no $HOME is set (asserts no
      `/home/<user>` fallback is taken).
    - `<lib>_home_for_user "$other_user"` returns failure when
      $other_user is not the running user, even if the regex would
      otherwise match.
    - `<lib>_home_for_user "$current_user"` succeeds with the
      sanitized $HOME, matching the prior behaviour for the only
      branch that's still allowed.
    - The `resolve_changelog_acfs_home` order swap: when both an
      explicit env var and a script-detected ACFS home exist, the
      detected one wins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -72,11 +72,6 @@ changelog_resolve_current_home() {
         fi
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
-        printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
-        return 0
-    fi
-

@@ -186,9 +182,13 @@ changelog_home_for_user() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
-        echo "/home/$user"
-        return 0
+    current_user="$(id -un 2>/dev/null || whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ "$user" == "$current_user" ]]; then
+        home_candidate="$(changelog_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$home_candidate" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$home_candidate"
+            return 0
+        fi
     fi
 
     return 1

+    candidate=$(changelog_script_acfs_home 2>/dev/null || true)
+    if [[ -n "$candidate" ]] && [[ -f "$candidate/state.json" || -f "$candidate/VERSION" || -f "$candidate/CHANGELOG.md" ]]; then
+        _CHANGELOG_RESOLVED_ACFS_HOME="$candidate"
         printf '%s\n' "$_CHANGELOG_RESOLVED_ACFS_HOME"
         return 0
     fi

         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
-        echo "/home/$user"
-        return 0
+    current_user="$(id -un 2>/dev/null || whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ "$user" == "$current_user" ]]; then
+        home_candidate="$(export_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$home_candidate" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$home_candidate"
+            return 0
+        fi
     fi
 
     return 1
 }
 
+infer_target_home_from_acfs_home() {
+    local acfs_home_candidate=""
+    local inferred_home=""
+
+    acfs_home_candidate="$(export_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${ACFS_HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$acfs_home_candidate" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$(basename "$acfs_home_candidate")" == ".acfs" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -f "$acfs_home_candidate/state.json" || -f "$acfs_home_candidate/VERSION" || -d "$acfs_home_candidate/onboard" ]] || return 1
+
+    inferred_home="${acfs_home_candidate%/.acfs}"
+    inferred_home="$(export_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$inferred_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$inferred_home" ]] || return 1
+
+    printf '%s\n' "$inferred_home"
+}
+
 prepare_target_context() {
     local detected_user=""
     local detected_home=""
@@ -401,6 +417,13 @@ prepare_target_context() {
             export TARGET_HOME="$detected_home"
         fi
     fi
+
+    if [[ -z "${TARGET_HOME:-}" ]]; then
+        detected_home=$(infer_target_home_from_acfs_home 2>/dev/null || true)
+        if [[ -n "$detected_home" ]]; then
+            export TARGET_HOME="$detected_home"
+        fi
+    fi
 }
 
 augment_path_for_target_user() {

         fi
     fi

-        return 0
-    fi

     return 1
 }

@@ -73,6 +69,13 @@ if [[ -z "$_SMOKE_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
     _SMOKE_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE="/var/lib/acfs/state.json"
 fi
 
+_smoke_script_acfs_home() {
+    local candidate=""

+}

 # Configuration

 fi

-        TARGET_HOME="/home/$TARGET_USER"
     else
         TARGET_HOME=""
     fi
@@ -222,6 +227,15 @@ _smoke_resolve_state_file() {
     local current_state_file=""
     local env_state_file=""
 
+    candidate="$(_smoke_script_acfs_home 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate" ]]; then
+        current_state_file="$candidate/state.json"
+        if [[ -f "$current_state_file" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$current_state_file"
+            return 0

+

         explicit_state_file="$_SMOKE_EXPLICIT_ACFS_HOME/state.json"
         if [[ -f "$explicit_state_file" ]]; then
@@ -260,6 +274,7 @@ _smoke_resolve_state_file() {

     printf '%s\n' "$candidate"

             fi
         fi
 
-        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
-            printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
-            return 0
+        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ ]]; then
+            home_candidate="$(eval "printf '%s\\n' ~$current_user" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+            home_candidate="$(state_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$home_candidate" 2>/dev/null || true)"

             resolved_home="$(state_resolve_current_home 2>/dev/null || true)"
             if [[ -n "$resolved_home" ]]; then
                 printf '%s\n' "$resolved_home"
                 return 0
             fi
         fi
 
-        if [[ "$TARGET_USER" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
-            printf '/home/%s\n' "$TARGET_USER"
-            return 0
-        fi

+        grep -RFn 'echo "/home/$user"' \
+            "$CONTINUE_SH" "$DASHBOARD_SH" "$INFO_SH" "$CHANGELOG_SH" "$EXPORT_CONFIG_SH" \
+            "$STATUS_SH" "$SUPPORT_SH" "$CHEATSHEET_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
+        grep -RFn "printf '/home/%s\\n' \"\\$TARGET_USER\"" "$STATE_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
+        grep -RFn 'TARGET_HOME="/home/$TARGET_USER"' "$SMOKE_TEST_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
+    } )"
+
+    if [[ -z "$output" ]]; then
+        harness_pass "runtime helpers do not guess home paths from usernames"
+    else
+        harness_fail "runtime helpers do not guess home paths from usernames" "$output"
+    fi
+}
